elements.sent-2411-001: We analyze essential properties about gravitational quantum field theory (GQFT) grounded on spin gauge symmetry by taking the general theory of quantum electrodynamics as example.
elements.sent-2411-002: A constraint equation for the field strength of gravigaiuge field is obtained to be as gravitization equation in spin-related gravigauge spacetime, which indicates how the gravitational effect emerges from non-commutative relation of gravigauge derivative operator.
elements.sent-2411-003: By representing the action in Minkowski spacetime, we show that translational invariance leads to zero energy momentum tensor in GQFT when applying for equations of motion for all basic fields including gravigauge field, which extends conservation law of energy momentum tensor in quantum field theory to cancellation law of energy momentum tensor in GQFT.
elements.sent-2411-004: An equivalence between general gravitational equation and zero energy momentum tensor is naturally resulted in GQFT.. ﻿.
elements.sent-2411-005: The framework of quantum field theory (QFT) was initially developed to describe the quantum electrodynamics (QED)\cite{QED1,QED2a,QED2b,QED3a,QED3b,QED3c,QED4a,QED4b , which combines successfully the quantum mechanics and special relativity and serves as a theoretical framework for describing microscopic world.}
elements.sent-2411-006: QFT forms the foundation of our comprehension on fundamental forces and elementary particles.
elements.sent-2411-007: In QFT, elementary particles as basic constituents of matter are referred to as quantum fields with specific intrinsic properties.
elements.sent-2411-008: Such a quantum field replaces Newton's point-like particle hypothesis that regards matter as dimensionless points without internal structure.
elements.sent-2411-009: The intrinsic properties of quantum field are characterized by quantum numbers, such as spin, electric charge, isospin and color-spin.
elements.sent-2411-010: It is those intrinsic quantum numbers that determine gauge symmetries which govern fundamental interactions of elementary particles along with gauge invariance principle.
elements.sent-2411-011: QFT provides a successful description for the three basic forces of electroweak and strong interactions in the standard model (SM)\cite{EW1,EW2,EW3,QCD1,QCD2,TW .}
elements.sent-2411-012: So far, QFT is highly effective in dealing with the microscopic world of elementary particles including their behavior and fundamental interactions at the quantum level.
elements.sent-2411-013: Einstein's general relativity (GR)\cite{GR,FGR was established to describe gravity.}
elements.sent-2411-014: So the gravity is no longer as an instantaneous long-range force, but as the dynamics of curved spacetime caused by mass and energy, which redefined our understanding of spacetime and gravitation by abandoning Newton's hypothesis of long-range instantaneous interactions.
elements.sent-2411-015: GR becomes very effective in describing the macroscopic world of gravitation and the behavior of massive objects within the framework of spacetime curvature on a large scale.
elements.sent-2411-016: Nevertheless, merging GR and QFT becomes a profound challenge as it is necessary to reconcile their fundamentally different viewpoints.
elements.sent-2411-017: To harmonize the contrasting essential notions inherent in these two theoretical frameworks, a quantum field theory of gravity was developed grounded on spin gauge symmetry SP(1,3) to provide a theoretical framework referred to as gravitational quantum field theory (GQFT)\cite{GQFT1,GQFT2 , which enables to treat the gravitational interaction on the same footing as the electroweak and strong interactions.}
elements.sent-2411-018: In GQFT, the essential notion to reconciling the foundational frameworks of GR and QFT lies in a fundamental principle that the laws of nature are governed by the intrinsic properties of basic constituents of matter.
elements.sent-2411-019: Such a principle requires a rigorous distinction between intrinsic symmetries, determined by the quantum numbers of elementary particles as quantum fields, and external symmetries, which characterize their motion in flat Minkowski spacetime of coordinates.This crucial distinction motivates us to conceptualize the so-called biframe spacetime with a fiber bundle structure.
elements.sent-2411-020: Where the globally flat Minkowski spacetime serves as a base spacetime, describing the free motion of elementary particles as quantum fields, and the spin-related intrinsic gravigauge spacetime acts as a fiber, regarded as an observable interacting spacetime charactering fundamental interactions among elementary particles.
elements.sent-2411-021: Such a conceptual framework exposes the distinguishable features between external and intrinsic symmetries, leading to a comprehensive understanding of the dynamics that governs the fundamental interactions of elementary particles as quantum fields.
elements.sent-2411-022: As a consequence, the global Lorentz symmetry SO(1,3) in Minkowski spacetime of coordinates and the intrinsic spin symmetry SP(1,3) in Hilbert space of Dirac spinor field become as joint symmetries SO(1,3)$\Join$SP(1,3) in GQFT rather than associated symmetries SO(1,3)$\adjoin$SP(1,3) in QFT.
elements.sent-2411-023: Here the spin symmetry SP(1,3) is localized as an intrinsic gauge symmetry along with the gauge invariance principle.
elements.sent-2411-024: To ensure the joint symmetries SO(1,3)$\Join$SP(1,3), a spin-related gravigauge field $\chi_{\mu ^{\; \, a (x)$ as bicovariant vector field defined in biframe spacetime is introduced as basic gravitational field instead of metric field in GR to describe the fundamental gravitational interaction.}}
elements.sent-2411-025: As GQFT framework provides a unified description on gravitational interaction and electroweak and strong interactions, it enables to build hyperunified field theory for unifying all fundamental interactions and elementary particles (see refs.\cite{HUFT1,HUFT2,FHUFT1,FHUFT2,FHUFT and references therein).}
elements.sent-2411-026: As the spin-related gravigauge field behaves as a Goldstone boson, it is identified to be massless graviton and its equation of motion is applied to describe gravidynamics within the framework of GQFT\cite{GQFT1,GQFT2 .}
elements.sent-2411-027: Recently, it was further demonstrated that in the absence of source fields, the linearized gravidynamics of gravigauge field leads to an interesting prediction on gravitational waves, namely there are five independent polarizations\cite{GQFT3 in GQFT instead of two polarizations of spin-2 mode in GR.}
elements.sent-2411-028: The extra three polarizations in GQFT arise from spin-0 mode with one polarization and spin-1 mode with two polarizations.
elements.sent-2411-029: The presence of spin-1 mode in GQFT implies the broken down of strong equivalence principle postulated in GR.
elements.sent-2411-030: In this paper, we are going to show further some essential properties in GQFT.
elements.sent-2411-031: A gravitization equation is resulted from the constraint equation for the field strength of gravigauge field in spin-related gravigauge spacetime.
elements.sent-2411-032: Such a field strength of gravigauge field reflects the group structure factor of non-commutative gravigauge derivative operator defined in gravigauge spacetime, which appears as an auxiliary field in the action of general theory of quantum electrodynamics formulated in local flat gravigauge spacetime.
elements.sent-2411-033: It is interesting to show that translational invariance in Minkowski spacetime leads to a cancellation law of energy momentum tensor in GQFT, which extends basically the conservation law of energy momentum tensor in QFT.
elements.sent-2411-034: As a consequence, we arrive at a general theorem on zero energy momentum tensor in entire Minkowski spacetime with the presence of gravitational interaction characterized by gravigauge field in GQFT.

elements.sent-2411-118: We investigate the essential properties of gravitational quantum field theory (GQFT) based on spin gauge symmetry, using the general theory of quantum electrodynamics as an example.
elements.sent-2411-119: A constraint equation for the field strength of the gravigauge field is derived, serving as a gravitization equation within the spin-related gravigauge spacetime.
elements.sent-2411-120: This equation reveals how gravitational effects emerge from the non-commutative relation of the gravigauge derivative operator.
elements.sent-2411-121: By transmuting the action from gravigauge spacetime to Minkowski spacetime, we demonstrate that translational invariance results in a vanishing energy-momentum tensor in GQFT when the equations of motion are applied to all fundamental fields, including the gravigauge field.
elements.sent-2411-122: This extends the conservation law of the energy-momentum tensor in quantum field theory to a cancellation law of the energy-momentum tensor in GQFT.
elements.sent-2411-123: As a result, an equivalence between the general gravitational equation and the zero energy-momentum tensor theorem naturally arises in GQFT.

elements.sent-2411-127: The framework of quantum field theory (QFT) was initially developed to describe quantum electrodynamics (QED) \cite{QED1,QED2a,QED2b,QED3a,QED3b,QED3c,QED4a,QED4b , successfully combining quantum mechanics and special relativity.}
elements.sent-2411-128: It serves as a theoretical foundation for describing the microscopic world.
elements.sent-2411-129: QFT underpins our understanding of fundamental forces and elementary particles.
elements.sent-2411-130: In this framework, elementary particles, as the basic constituents of matter, are represented as quantum fields with specific intrinsic properties.
elements.sent-2411-131: This concept replaces Newton's hypothesis of point-like particles, which treated matter as dimensionless points without internal structure.
elements.sent-2411-132: The intrinsic properties of quantum fields are characterized by quantum numbers, such as spin, electric charge, isospin, and color-spin.
elements.sent-2411-133: These quantum numbers determine the gauge symmetries that govern the fundamental interactions of elementary particles, in accordance with the principle of gauge invariance.
elements.sent-2411-134: QFT has successfully described the three fundamental forces, electroweak and strong interactions, within the Standard Model (SM) \cite{EW1,EW2,EW3,QCD1,QCD2,TW , based on gauge field theories \cite{GT1,GT2 .}}
elements.sent-2411-135: To date, QFT has proven highly effective in addressing the microscopic world of elementary particles, including their behavior and fundamental interactions at the quantum level.
elements.sent-2411-136: Einstein's general relativity (GR) \cite{GR,FGR was established to describe gravity.}
elements.sent-2411-137: In this theory, gravity is no longer treated as an instantaneous long-range force but rather as the dynamics of curved spacetime caused by mass and energy.
elements.sent-2411-138: This revolutionary framework redefined our understanding of spacetime and gravitation by abandoning Newton's hypothesis of long-range instantaneous interactions.
elements.sent-2411-139: GR has also proven highly effective in describing the macroscopic world of gravitation and the behavior of massive objects within the framework of spacetime curvature on large scales.
elements.sent-2411-140: Nevertheless, merging GR and QFT presents a profound challenge, as it requires reconciling their fundamentally different perspectives.
elements.sent-2411-141: To harmonize the contrasting notions inherent in these two theoretical frameworks, a quantum field theory of gravity was developed based on the spin gauge symmetry SP(1,3).
elements.sent-2411-142: This provides a theoretical foundation referred to as Gravitational Quantum Field Theory (GQFT) \cite{GQFT1,GQFT2 , which treats gravitational interactions on the same footing as electroweak and strong interactions.}
elements.sent-2411-143: In GQFT, the key to reconciling GR and QFT lies in a fundamental principle: the laws of nature are governed by the intrinsic properties of the basic constituents of matter.
elements.sent-2411-144: This principle necessitates a rigorous distinction between intrinsic symmetries, determined by the quantum numbers of elementary particles as quantum fields, and external symmetries, which describe their motion in the flat Minkowski spacetime of coordinates.
elements.sent-2411-145: This crucial distinction motivates the conceptualization of a bi-frame spacetime with a fiber bundle structure.
elements.sent-2411-146: Here, the globally flat Minkowski spacetime serves as the base spacetime, describing the free motion of elementary particles as quantum fields, while the spin-related intrinsic gravigauge spacetime acts as the fiber, representing an observable interacting spacetime that characterizes fundamental interactions among elementary particles.
elements.sent-2411-147: This conceptual framework highlights the distinguishable features between external and intrinsic symmetries, leading to a comprehensive understanding of the dynamics governing the fundamental interactions of elementary particles as quantum fields.
